claim: Israeli forces conducted extensive airstrikes on Iranian sites including IRGC facilities, missile sites, and petrochemical plants, killing IRGC intelligence chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi.
Iran's IRGC vowed 'Operation Crushing Revenge' against Israel in response to Khademi's assassination.
UAE intercepted 14 missiles and 19 drones from Iran in a 24-hour period, with 4 people wounded.
Saudi Arabia downed 7 ballistic missiles from Iranian attacks.
Jordan reported one Iranian missile and drone impacting its territory, wounding 2 people.
US military conducted strikes on Iranian-backed Iraqi militias targeting an intelligence headquarters and base near Haliwa Airport.
President Trump issued a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday night or face strikes on power plants and infrastructure.
Iran rejected a US ceasefire proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz on April 6.
Pakistan proposed a two-week truce and extension of Trump's deadline, which Iran is positively reviewing.
A 15-point US peace plan was delivered to Iran via Pakistan covering Strait reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Tehran is reviewing the US peace plan but insists on no direct negotiations.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in late February US-Israel strikes.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed as successor to Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei by the Assembly of Experts.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline ex-IRGC deputy, was appointed chief of the Supreme National Security Council.
President Masoud Pezeshkian accused IRGC commanders of wrecking ceasefire chances through unilateral actions.
Hardliners branded ex-Foreign Minister Zarif a 'traitor' for calling to end the war, urging his arrest.
Iran executed at least five men last week on anti-protest and security charges, including protester Amirhossein Hatami.
Recent protests in Iran marked the most serious unrest ever faced by the regime.
Intensive strikes on Monday killed 49 civilians and injured 58, hitting power-related targets ahead of Trump's deadline.
Trump reported productive talks via envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner with a top Iranian official, postponing energy strikes for five days.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied that negotiations occurred with the US.
Mojtaba Khamenei warned adversaries' security must be removed.
IRGC spokesperson threatened tourist centers worldwide in response to Israeli strikes.
